reviews.review_text: No water available on sites, rabbit dung hills everywhere, only two showers and two toilets available in the ladies, roof falling in on one of the showers, just give it a miss if you can, $45 a night for not much.

Nice park for our overnight stay, right on the beach and close to town.  The perfect location. Amenities clean and tidy.||Unfortunately the owners are selling due to lack of patrons, and the park is closing.

I’ve stayed in a motel cabin here 3 consecutive years & loved it! Cheryl & Graham are sincerely friendly & accommodating, the cabins are clean, comfortable & peaceful, & the location on the ocean at the river mouth is brilliant!  The accommodation is also great value for money. I highly recommend staying at Beach Retreat!|Luke 😊

I’ve stayed in a motel cabin here 3 consecutive years & loved it! Cheryl & Graham are sincerely friendly & accommodating, the cabins are clean, comfortable & peaceful, & the location on the ocean at the river mouth is brilliant!  The accommodation is also great value for money. I highly recommend staying at Beach Retreat!
Luke

We booked this park for the location. Unaware that the water to each caravan site has been cut off. We should have been advised of this by the managers of the park on booking or in our conversation prior to a late arrival at the park. ||The amenities block is disgusting. Old concrete floors, old toilets & shower heads with stainless steel shower bases. Two of the showers are locked with only 2 open for use. It’s dark & dingy inside the shower cubical with laser light covered with dirt & grime. If water is of such prime importance I don’t understand why the continual leaking toilet in the ladies hasn’t been fixed. It’s obvious that there has been no maintenance done for many years. ||We have travelled on the mainland many times and as a Tasmanian I’m ashamed that this is what travellers experience when coming to our beautiful state. ||Suggestions:  Don’t stay here.
Spectacular location, natural setting right on the beach, very close to shops and restaurants, friendly and welcoming staff, quirky decor. We had a lovely time.

We were here for one night only but the campsite is really nice situated on the beach. You walk to a great playground only 5-7min. A boardwalk is starting from the campsite and the facilities are really clean. You have no water at the campsite but the host offers you to fill up your tank if needed.

Positives: the owner was friendly & helpful as we checked in. Negatives: It's a little rundown (there is a lot of broken concrete in areas), we were told the water wasn't working in the park(should have checked the reviews beforehand, apparently an ongoing issue), the ablution block is basic but clean, the park has no lighting so bring a torch for after dark. The camp kitchen is clean but basic as well, there is no oven either, not even a basic toaster oven. Being $45 a night for a site, it's a bit expensive for what is offered compared to the rest of Tas. Needs a lot of renovation.

Had two nights booked but decided to cancel due to really bad weather. I was met with a grumpy lady who was clearly disappointed in the cancellation (because I assume everyone else was also cancelling also). She was just flat out rude, took my details & said goodbye. |I called on the Wednesday for our Friday & Saturday night, so not exactly last minute for a caravan park.|This review is not based on the site itself but deserves 2 stars to customer service.
We have been here before, Cheryl is a lovely lady

Terribly Expensive
Nice location, friendly staff
Unfortunately, no water, no BBQ, no lights.
Kitchen did not have the basics necessary to cook.
No bins, except for a tiny 1litre flip bin in the toilet.
No paper towel, no dryer, and some dead communal soap.
Unable to get to the toilet blocks in the dark, path has foliage spilling over, corner post not visible and no lighting.
One key to toilets for a family

Massive shoutout to the Wynyard Beach Retreat Tourist Park’s Cheryl & Graeme who accommodated my family after our motorhome lost power on the Bass Highway.

Cheryl waited up for us after hours, helped unload our taxi, provided boxes for our gear, helped move us in for the night & gave us a late checkout until our replacement vehicle arrived this afternoon.

Rooms were just what we needed neat, warm, comfortable & very clean.  If your lap takes you this way make sure you book a night.  I am sure the friendly face of Cheryl will be there to welcome you.

Thank you for being a good human and great operator.

NOT TO BE MISSED! Do yourself a favour and spend time here. $40 a night for solo. They win points for a solo discount alone!!
Very friendly and helpful managers/owners.
Everything spotlessly clean!! And as for the effort they've put in for homeliness...I'm speechless. I've never seen anything like it in all my stays in caravan parks! I hope I see more CP adopting these ideas.
Concrete sites leaving grassed area to sit on and enjoy. Feel that grass beneath your bare feet!
Great pressure in the showers. Shower is to die for! Very clean! Large toilet cubicles.
AND THEN THERE IS THE KITCHEN AND LOUNGE!! You have to see it!
A pathway separates you from the beach.
There is a BBQ area with an open log fireplace, sink x 2 and picnic table and benches.
Seriously I've never seen anything remotely as good as this place.
People complain that there is no water at your site but there is the opportunity to fill your tank etc at reception.  That is not a valid complaint people!
Fantastic location- lovely low key park with nice touches, great river walk, beach, nearby places to see. A real haven , loved it. A must stay- base yourself here and go to Stanley.

Fabulous park for peace and serenity with lovely owners. Ocean views/access on your doorstep. Fishing, swimming and easy walk into town. No water at your powered sites but access to it if required. If this is for you then don’t go passed 👌👌👌👌

One of the best locations to spend time to see and experience  this end of the Coast. A quiet safe river and seaside location off the main highway with beautiful clean gardens, parks with aquatic activities and all within one hours drive of the Spirit of Tasmania one way and Stanley to the West for day trips. Also a gateway to the West Coast. Excellent self contained cabins and units. Caravan,  Camper and Camping sites have access to amenities and kitchen. Also dog friendly stay with a dog beach directly in front of the Park.
Such a beautiful location, quiet, but close to town and friendly service 😊
Stayed on unpowered site, lovely location right by the beach and close to town centre. Clean bathrooms and kitchen with fridge. Lovely staff!

Maybe the bathroom being cleaned 5 days ago was the last time it was done? very dated grotty ammenities with prison toilets, too low shower heads and no way to dry hands. Owners rant on checking in included free camping ruining her park, covid and councils. We were placed on the main walk way to the beach despite booking in with our dog and many other sites available. Need a key for toilets and were chased down to return it this morning despite having not checked out yet. Water access continues to be an issue. One washing machine and dryer which are locked at 6pm.

A very clean bathroom and very friendly staff. A night of 40 includes water, electricity, bathroom, shower, and kitchen, which I think is very worthwhile. The surrounding environment is also very good, a very beautiful town with supermarkets, I think it's suitable to pause.

The room we had was nothing but a dump. The toilet system ran all night. There  were spiders on the curtains in the bedroom. A one bar heater was the only form of heat for 3 rooms and a bathroom. The front sliding wire door fell off. The floor was dirty. There were plastic garden chairs stacked up for dinning chairs. The 2 seater couch was like it come from the tip. We paid for 3 nights but left after the first night. I don’t like spiders  so I never slept. There was no one in Reception when we decided to leave so we have left various messages and calls to which we have had no reply. We have travelled Australia and this has been the very worst accommodation and owner we have ever come across. We are Tasmanians and support our communities by staying in cabins and hotels. It was dreadfully over priced for such a horrible cabin and not getting a reply from the owner isn’t satisfactory at all.

We recently had two nights stay at the tourist Park, and if I could give a minus review this would be it. From the moment we booked in the woman was abrupt and should’ve had warning then. My husband and I went out for a walk one day and on our return we realized that some one had been in the cabin. Can only assume it was the owner. When you rent a property technically anyone entering is doing so unlawfully. Would not recommend this park to anyone, no internet, cluttered cabin and no air con.
Great location,  on the beach and close to town . Aswome couple of days away , thankyou.

This place was the worst! |Been there a few times now to help support the park but the owners are not very welcoming people. They have turned off the water to the individual sites to save money, saying the have a mystery ‘water leak’. Rubbish, it’s been the same way for many months, but mysteriously the water to their private place and the kitchen and toilet blocks somehow have water….amazing.|Also, god forbid parking your van on the grass. Apparently the concrete slab is only for parking your van on, not on the grass bedside it? Keep off the grass or leave the park and go elsewhere. That’s what we had to do. Give this place a ‘big miss’. Beautiful location but hopeless at making you feel welcome.|Patto |Port Sorell
Stayed 2 nights, spotless cabin, owner delightful and could not do enough for us. No noise and a short stroll through park to beach. Would not go elsewhere in Wynyard.

Quaint, maybe; clean, yes; attractive, okay; location, excellent; quality, fair. It's a place to settle for a night and be okay. Maybe the taps drip, but there are better and worse places around. There is no UHT milk, they bring you real milk. If you need a bed, it's great. Value is just okay. It is sited next to the busiest recreation trails I have seen and great to use.

A wonderful location on the waterfront at Wynard. We didn't feel welcome. So many rules and a grumpy male manager. Paid $45 but had no water on site or anywhere
I  the campground. Found out taos had been turned off because the water supply was too expensive. A reduction in the fee would have been appropriate! The showers and toilets were ancient. Though clean,  old concrete gloves left damp never feel clean. The lighting was minimal, and quite scary in the dark. So sorry but not a great place.

We have just stayed 2x nights in our campervan and what a lovely park . Quite small so not overcrowded the owner made us feel very welcome the facilities while a little outdated were very clean and well maintained

The location is excellent...right on the beach with a nice boardwalk which takes you to town in minutes along the Riverwalk. So eateries and pubs are walking distance. It's a small, quaint, quiet caravan park with lovely kept gardens, nice decorative touches to the toilet/shower facility and large enclosed kitchen/tv lounge area. Great for dogs with the beach, paths and huge park nearby. It has everything I could ever ask for and I love it...staying a week😊❤️
Old and very run down. Drove in and drove straight out again
